quoteFirst: "Why don't you say that the Comanches passed this spot at precisely
quoteLast: o'clock this morning? You might just as well be particular, Jerry."
title:
The Young Trail Hunters
Or, the Wild Riders of the Plains. The Veritable Adventures of Hal Hyde and Ned Brown, on Their Journey Across the Great Plains of the South-West
author: Cozzens, Samuel Woodworth, 1834-1878

quoteLast: by firing seven salvoes at a ship of the _Kaiser_ class, then engaged a cruiser, and again a battleship, and at 6.54 she was hit by a torpedo and took up a considerable list to starboard, but we opened at 7.3 p.m. at a cruiser and at 7.12 p.m. fired fourteen rapid salvoes at a ship of the _Koenig_ class, hitting her frequently until she turned out of the line.
title: World's War Events, Vol. II
author: Reynolds, Francis J. (Francis Joseph), 1867-1937 [Editor]; Churchill, Allen L. (Allen Leon), 1873-1940 [Editor]

quoteFirst: The first point essential to an understanding is that Jellicoe’s deployment was not complete until late in the afternoon,
quoteLast: G.M.T., that the evening was misty, and the “visibility” poor.
title:
The Silent Watchers
England's Navy during the Great War: What It Is, and What We Owe to It
author: Copplestone, Bennet, 1867-1932

quoteLast: by firing seven salvoes at a ship of     the “Kaiser” class, then engaged a cruiser, and again a battleship,     and at 6.54 she was hit by a torpedo and took up a considerable     list to starboard, but re-opened at 7.3 P.M. at a cruiser and at     7.12 P.M. fired fourteen rapid salvoes at a ship of the “König”     class, hitting her frequently until she turned out of the line.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteLast: On reaching home I went up to     dress to my own room, which communicates with my wife’s by a     stained oak door.
title: The Puppet Show of Memory

quoteFirst: The action between the battle fleets lasted intermittently from
quoteLast: to 8.20 P.M. at ranges between 9,000 and 12,000 yards,     during which time the British Fleet made alterations of course from     S.E. by E. to W. in the endeavour to close.
title: The Grand Fleet, 1914-1916
author: Jellicoe, John Rushworth, 1859-1935

quoteFirst: The First Battle Squadron, under Vice-Admiral Sir Cecil Burney, came into action at
quoteLast: with the enemy's Third Battle Squadron, at a range of about 11,000 yards, and administered severe punishment, both to the battleships and to the battle-cruisers and light-cruisers, which were also engaged.
title: World's War Events, Vol. II
author: Reynolds, Francis J. (Francis Joseph), 1867-1937 [Editor]; Churchill, Allen L. (Allen Leon), 1873-1940 [Editor]
